claim: A passport belonging to Satam al-Suqami — one of the five hijackers aboard American Airlines Flight 11 (which struck WTC 1 at 8:46 a.m.) — was recovered in lower Manhattan and given to NYPD Detective Yuk H. Chin by an unidentified passerby (a male in a business suit, ~30 years old) moments before WTC 2 collapsed at 9:59 a.m. The detective handed the passport to the FBI on 9/11. In her January 2004 testimony to the 9/11 Commission, lead counsel Susan Ginsburg stated the passport had been 'manipulated in a fraudulent manner' in a pattern associated with al-Qaeda travel-document forgery.

sources.label: The 9/11 Commission, “Final Report of the National Commission on Terrorist Attacks Upon the United States” (2004), pp. 1 and 451; Staff Statement and testimony of Susan Ginsburg (Jan 26, 2004)
Wikipedia — “Satam al-Suqami”
sources.quote: Al-Suqami's passport was found by a passerby in the vicinity of Vesey Street

sources.label: Haley Press / @strategichaley, Instagram reel DVOtv6QCTFf (Feb 26, 2026) — surfacing source

notes: 9/11 Commission Report is the primary government record on the passport recovery and Ginsburg's testimony about document manipulation. URL pending — canonical at govinfo.gov or 9-11commission.gov. The reel's framing treats the passport's survival as too convenient; the recovery is documented but the inference of plant/conspiracy is contested. Three other hijackers' passports also wholly or partially survived (Jarrah, Saeed al-Ghamdi at Flight 93 site; Abdulaziz al-Omari in luggage that didn't make Flight 11).
